name: on-grief-and-grieving
description: Elisabeth Kübler-Ross and David Kessler's 'On Grief and Grieving: Finding the Meaning of Grief Through the Five Stages of Loss' — the definitive guide to grief. Explains the five stages (denial, anger, bargaining, depression, acceptance) as non-linear responses to loss. Covers the inner world of grief, the outer world of grief, specific circumstances (children, suicide, disaster), and both authors' personal experiences with loss.
useCase: Understanding the grieving process, the five stages as non-linear responses, navigating the inner and outer experiences of grief, supporting someone who is grieving, and finding meaning after loss.
knownRisks: The five stages are often misunderstood as linear or universal
Personal experiences with grief vary widely — not a prescription
May trigger intense emotions for those currently grieving
Not a substitute for professional grief counseling
